macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Stocks Rally for Second Day as Tech Leads Gains
items.summary: The S&P 500 advanced 0.8% to 6,946.13, Nasdaq Composite surged 1.3% to 23,152.08, and Dow Jones rose 0.6% to 49,482.15 as investors returned to growth stocks following eased AI concerns. Microsoft gained 3.0% and Palantir jumped 4.2%.

items.title: Nvidia Beats Earnings Estimates with 75% Data Center Growth
items.summary: Nvidia reported earnings that beat expectations and announced 75% data center growth, though the stock was little changed after hours. The results are being closely watched as a test of continued market momentum.

items.title: Salesforce Slides on Softer Revenue Guidance
items.summary: Salesforce declined approximately 4% after providing softer revenue guidance, offsetting broader market strength in the technology sector.

items.title: European Markets Hit Records on Tariff Relief
items.summary: European equities reached record levels as investors responded positively to U.S. tariff outcomes appearing less severe than feared. HSBC jumped 7.9% on strong 2025 results and improved outlook, while ASML gained 2.0% amid continued AI investment focus.

items.title: Circle Internet Group Surges 35.5% on Strong Stablecoin Demand
items.summary: Circle Internet Group jumped 35.5% after reporting results that beat expectations, driven by strong demand for stablecoins as digital assets attempt to stabilize.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Stabilize After Recent Pressure
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $68,176 and Ethereum near $2,063 as digital assets showed cautious recovery. The broader tone reflects stabilization rather than full risk-on enthusiasm.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Rebounds to $68,000 Amid Dip Buying and Risk-On Sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin surged from a year-to-date low of $60,000 to $68,000 as investors bought the dip, with futures open interest hitting $45 billion and spot ETFs seeing inflows. Analysts warn of potential dead-cat bounce risk after recent drop from all-time high of $126,200.

items.title: Global Crypto Market Cap Rises 3.44% to $2.26 Trillion
items.summary: The total cryptocurrency market capitalization increased by 3.44% over the last 24 hours to $2.26 trillion, reflecting broad market recovery amid Bitcoin's rebound.

items.title: STS Digital Raises $30 Million from CMT Digital and Kraken for Crypto Trading Platform
items.summary: Bermuda-based STS Digital, targeting institutional investors, secured $30 million in funding to expand options contracts on over 400 cryptocurrencies despite market downturn. The platform is onboarding larger institutional clients amid rising demand for derivatives.

items.title: Ethereum Shows Upward Impulse After Downward Trend
items.summary: Ethereum prices, in a recent downward trend with low volatility, experienced a clear upward movement in yesterday's session.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: DataJoint Launches Agentic AI Control Layer for Scientific Workflows
items.summary: DataJoint announced DataJoint Agentic AI, a governed execution layer enabling semi-autonomous AI operation on structured scientific data for reproducible workflows in regulated R&D environments like pharma and biotech.

items.title: Anthropic Acquires Vercept for Advanced Agentic AI Tools
items.summary: Anthropic acquired Seattle-based startup Vercept, specializing in AI agents that interact with software like humans for tasks such as data entry and debugging, amid fierce talent competition in AI.

items.title: MIT Develops Method to Boost LLM Training Efficiency by Up to 210%
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced a technique using computational downtime and adaptive speculative decoding to accelerate reasoning LLM training by 70-210% while maintaining accuracy, producing a deployable smaller drafter model as a byproduct.

items.title: Accenture and Mistral AI Announce Multi-Year Collaboration for Enterprise AI
items.summary: Accenture and Mistral AI launched a strategic partnership to co-develop scalable, enterprise-grade AI solutions for secure deployments across industries, leveraging Mistral's models and Accenture's expertise.

items.title: Agentic AI 'Einstein' Completes Entire Online Courses, Sparking Education Debate
items.summary: Companion's Einstein AI agent demonstrated completing full online courses on Canvas, raising concerns about cheating, learning integrity, and the need for guardrails in higher education.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Towerborne 1.0 Launches on Multiple Platforms Including Game Pass
items.summary: Stoic's action RPG Towerborne reaches full 1.0 release today, February 26, 2026, on Xbox, PC, PS5, and available day-one on Game Pass Ultimate, PC, and Premium with cross-platform play and co-op support.[3]

items.title: Bungie’s Marathon Preview Amid Streaming Controversy and March 5 Release
items.summary: Bungie hosted a free preview for its upcoming extraction shooter Marathon, set for March 5, 2026 on PC, PS5, and Xbox, generating buzz and controversy involving streamer Dr Disrespect.[1]

items.title: Invincible VS Reveals Powerplex as Next Playable Character
items.summary: Skybound Entertainment and 5th Planet Games announced Powerplex, a former GDA scientist turned villain, as the next playable character in the 3v3 tag-fighting game Invincible VS, launching April 30, 2026.[2]

items.title: Minimap Hosts First Indie Game Showcase During Steam Next Fest
items.summary: Minimap is hosting its inaugural Indie Game Showcase on February 26, 2026, as part of the opening week of Steam Next Fest, highlighting upcoming indie titles.[4]

items.title: UK Advertising Regulator Targets Loot Boxes in Mobile Games
items.summary: The UK ad regulator issued an enforcement notice requiring game and advertising companies to increase transparency about loot boxes in mobile titles.[6]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Launches Galaxy S26 Series Smartphones
items.summary: Samsung unveiled its new Galaxy S26, S26 Plus, and S26 Ultra models in San Francisco, positioning them as competitors to Apple's iPhone 17 lineup with enhanced features including improved Bixby capabilities.

items.title: IonQ Deploys Romania's National Quantum Communication Infrastructure
items.summary: IonQ has successfully launched one of Europe's largest terrestrial quantum key distribution (QKD) networks in Romania, marking a major advancement in quantum-secure communications.

items.title: Nvidia Receives License to Ship H200 Chips to China
items.summary: Nvidia has been granted a license to export some H200 chips to China amid ongoing U.S. export policy changes, though full resolution on sales remains uncertain.

items.title: Google Acquires Robotics Firm Intrinsic
items.summary: Google has brought the robotics software company Intrinsic fully in-house, ending its status as an independent Alphabet subsidiary.

items.title: Salesforce Reports 12% Revenue Growth in Earnings
items.summary: Salesforce announced a 12% increase in revenue for the quarter, though shares declined following the results amid concerns over AI disruption in software.

items.title: C3.ai Announces 26% Workforce Layoffs
items.summary: C3.ai plans to cut 26% of its workforce following a CEO transition, as part of broader cost-cutting measures.
